We’ve covered the trend in detail, and at this year’s TechCrunch Disrupt event, we want to help pre-seed founders now being held to seed-stage […]

articles.title: Zoox issues software recall after a robotaxi got confused by heavy smoke
articles.description: The recall comes as the top automotive safety regulator in the U.S. has warned AV companies about their vehicles interfering with first responders.

articles.title: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
articles.description: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

articles.title: San Francisco mayor pushes for tougher rules after the Waymo traffic fiasco
articles.description: In the wake of a massive hours-long gridlock event, San Francisco mayor Daniel Lurie has told state regulators it's time to put more requirements on robotaxi operators like Waymo.

articles.title: SpaceX suddenly aborts second Starship V3 launch after ignition
articles.description: The company didn't immediately say what went wrong. SpaceX's stock plunged more than 4% in after-hours trading before paring losses.

articles.title: Coca-Cola suspended production at its Fairlife dairy after a ransomware attack
articles.description: Coca Cola said dairy production at its Fairlife unit will "remain suspended" in the United States following a hack.

articles.title: Founders Fund hires former OpenAI exec Ryan Beiermeister (and not because of her ‘Mafia’ skills)
articles.description: Ryan Beiermeister, who demonstrated cool analysis in the Founders Fund YouTube series "Mafia," has joined the firm as a partner.

articles.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
articles.description: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

articles.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
articles.description: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.

articles.title: Oil giant BP shutters its corporate venture arm after 20 years
articles.description: BP Ventures is shutting down, ending a nearly 20-year run that was marked by reportedly lackluster returns.

articles.title: Uber’s $14.8B Delivery Hero deal would nearly double its global footprint
articles.description: Uber has agreed to acquire Delivery Hero in a $14.8 billion all-stock deal that would nearly double the company’s global footprint and create one of the world’s largest food-delivery platforms outside China.

articles.title: X cracks down on creators who steal content

articles.title: Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps

articles.title: Google continues its renaming streak by turning NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook

articles.title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
articles.description: DoorDash is opening a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool that lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal, marking another step toward software designed for AI agents instead of just humans.

articles.title: UK cops say arrest of two young hackers disrupted the operations of an infamous hacking group
articles.description: Owen Flowers and Thalha Jubair, two members of the prolific Scattered Spider hacking group, pleaded guilty and were sentenced to five years and six months in jail for hacking London’s metropolitan transit system.

articles.title: Period tracker Stardust shares users’ health data with analytics firm, says Mozilla research
articles.description: One period tracker app tested by Mozilla was 'squeaky clean,' while another app was seen sharing users' health data with an analytics company, underscoring vast differences in user privacy among these apps.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **自动驾驶事故与监管收紧**：Zoox因机器人在浓烟中行驶混乱而发布软件召回；旧金山市长因Waymo交通事件推动更严格的规则制定。
2. **AI与创意工具升级**：Google Vids允许用户主演AI视频；Roblox在移动应用中推出AI驱动的游戏创建功能；AI旅行平台Fora获6000万美元融资成为独角兽。
3. **太空与运输领域动态**：SpaceX在点火后突然中止第二次星舰V3发射；Uber以148亿美元收购Delivery Hero，全球业务版图近乎翻倍。
4. **网络安全与商业调整**：可口可乐因勒索软件攻击暂停Fairlife乳制品生产；英国石油BP关闭运营20年的企业风投部门。
5. **内容平台与融资新趋势**：X平台打击创作者盗用内容；Beehiiv推出订阅者聊天功能并融入AI；Founders Fund雇佣前OpenAI高管，预示AI人才争夺升级。

**今日最值得关注的发展：** 自动驾驶领域接连出现安全与监管问题，同时AI在内容创作、游戏和旅行等消费场景的渗透加速，显示出技术落地与风险治理并行的行业格局。

articles.title: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
articles.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none of them use today; a majority intend to switch or add providers within the year, many within a quarter. Buying decisions turn on integration and total cost of ownership rather than headline token price — which is fortunate, because most enterprises cannot yet see their unit economics clearly: GPUs sit at half utilizati

articles.title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
articles.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents. The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agents, spending remains a thin slice of the security budget, and enterprises are evenly split on whethe

articles.title: The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
articles.description: Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector databases that define the category — yet a majority of enterprises have already watched their agents produce confident, wrong answers traced to missing or inconsistent context. A governed semantic layer is emerging as the fix, but most are still building it; the field is converging on hybrid retrieval; and even as provider

articles.title: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
articles.description: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully trusts automated evaluation today; and the most-cited weakness is that evaluations do not align with real-world outcomes. Yet two-thirds already allow, or are actively engineering toward, deploying agent changes to production on automated evaluation alone — with no human in the loop. The result is an evaluation gap —

articles.title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
articles.description: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: most deployed “agents” are still chatbot wrappers, the control plane enterprises expect is deliberately hybrid to avoid lock-in, and real-time fiscal control over token burn remains the exception. This wave of VentureBeat Pulse Research examines enterprise agent orchestration: which platforms enterprises run on, what dri

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **NVIDIA Nemotron-3嵌入模型登顶RTEB排行榜**：该模型在检索增强与智能体任务中表现最佳，推动智能体检索技术发展。
2. **OpenAI发布AI时代评分卡与安全倡议**：包括AI安全评分卡、青少年安全AI使用指南，以及通过州与联邦行动推进AI安全。
3. **谷歌视觉搜索创新25周年与AI视频功能更新**：回顾视觉搜索发展历程，并推出Vids视频创建、编辑及主角生成功能。
4. **模型路由的复杂性与智能体构建经验**：探讨模型路由的实际挑战，并分享构建智能体（如Shippy项目）的实践经验。
5. **多款AI工具与安全事件披露**：包括Thinking Machines发布Inkling、VoiceEQ评估语音AI人声质量、Cars24用OpenAI优化对话，以及Hugging Face披露2026年7月安全事件。

**最值得关注**：NVIDIA Nemotron-3在核心检索任务上取得突破性进展，可能重新定义智能体系统的信息获取方式。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **中国AI战略与全球领导地位**：习近平主席多次强调中国在人工智能领域的全球领导目标，包括在上海论坛上阐述AI外交愿景，并推动中国成为新全球AI秩序的主导者，挑战美国主导地位。同时，中国AI公司Z.ai有望成为首家年销售额达10亿美元的国内AI企业。
2. **中美关系与经济博弈**：特朗普指责中国“选举盗窃”，中方则积极展现AI领导力。研究显示，西方若减少对中国依赖将损失23万亿美元。此外，中国电动汽车产业被视为对西方车企的“海盗式”冲击，而中国股市单日蒸发4.3万亿人民币引发关注。
3. **AI监管与社会影响**：中国拟禁止AI情感陪伴（“AI恋爱”），同时全球AI审查机制在中国、印度、美国、欧洲等地接受测试。中国还举办了首场机器人综合格斗赛事，展示技术应用新场景。
4. **国际形象与地缘技术竞争**：多国民众对中国的好感度超过美国。中俄是否具备摧毁“星链”的能力引发讨论，中国玩具批发等传统贸易模式仍受关注。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：习近平系统提出中国AI全球领导战略，既展现技术雄心，也凸显中美在人工智能领域的主导权竞争加剧。
